medical_history.chief_complaint: numbness and burning pain initially confined to the toes and the balls of her feet
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: 3-year history of numbness and burning pain initially confined to the toes and the balls of her feet. When walking, she felt as though she had rocks in her shoes, and she preferred to walk barefoot. She developed marked allodynia, shooting pains in her feet and legs, and painful calf cramps. These symptoms slowly spread proximally to involve the whole foot and distal calf.

physical_examination.general_examination: sensitivity to pinprick and temperature was diminished on the feet and distal calves. She had normal strength, reflexes (including ankle jerks), vibratory sensitivity, and proprioception.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Results of nerve conduction studies were normal. QST thermal thresholds to cooling were abnormally elevated both at the foot (96th percentile) and hand (>99th percentile), whereas vibratory thresholds were normal. Skin biopsy specimens showed reduced IENF densities more marked in skin from distal than proximal sites (IENF densities: calf skin: 0 mm-’ [normal 2 3.81; thigh skin: 12.5 mm-' [normal 2 5.21). A sural nerve biopsy specimen taken from the calf was normal at the light microscopic level.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Reduced IENF densities in skin from both proximal and distal sites. Absolute IENF densities in calf skin were reduced below the lower limit of normal (5th percentile) in 26 (81%).
4 had selective loss of small myelinated and/or unmyelinated axons and 2 had normal histology and fiber densities despite reduced IENF densities in skin biopsy specimens.

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Quantitative sensory testing (QST) for thermal thresholds
laboratory_examinations.findings: Abnormal in some patients, indicating small sensory fiber dysfunction.
May be abnormal in SFSN, indicating co-involvement of small diameter sensory and autonomic nerve fibers.

other_tests.findings: Normal in all cases, indicating no large myelinated fiber involvement.

Morphological abnormalities of cutaneous innervation, including reduced intraepidermal nerve fiber density, predegenerative nerve terminal swellings, and segmented degenerating dermal and epidermal fibers.

other_tests.procedure_name: Quantitative sensory testing (QST) for vibration and cooling
other_tests.findings: Sensory thresholds equal to or greater than the 95th percentile for age were considered abnormal.
